scenarios.expected_degradation: Writes reject or retry before transaction; reads may degrade.
scenarios.user_visible_effect: Transient API errors and delayed realtime delivery.
scenarios.data_safety: Committed rows and outbox events remain authoritative; no event-before-commit.

scenarios.expected_degradation: New pooled work is rejected; direct recovery capacity remains reserved.
scenarios.user_visible_effect: Retryable command errors and stale queries.
scenarios.data_safety: No command is acknowledged without commit.

scenarios.expected_degradation: Realtime delivery delays while durable writes continue within admission limits.
scenarios.user_visible_effect: Clients observe delayed events and use resume/gap repair.
scenarios.data_safety: Outbox rows are retained until idempotent terminal handoff.

scenarios.expected_degradation: Background work ages while command/realtime planes retain priority.
scenarios.data_safety: Domain outbox semantics are unaffected by Oban backlog.

scenarios.expected_degradation: Ephemeral messages coalesce/drop; slow sessions disconnect before unbounded queues.
scenarios.data_safety: Durable events remain repairable from stable sequence/cursor.

scenarios.expected_degradation: Admission throttles reattachment while command plane remains available.
scenarios.user_visible_effect: Up to 25 percent of sessions reconnect within 60 seconds.
scenarios.data_safety: Resume and gap repair produce zero durable event loss.

scenarios.expected_degradation: Feature flag selects pure Elixir reference implementation.
scenarios.user_visible_effect: Latency may increase within declared fallback envelope.
scenarios.data_safety: Native code remains acceleration only; durable authority is unchanged.

scenarios.expected_degradation: Panic maps to bounded error and opens native fallback/circuit guard.
scenarios.user_visible_effect: Affected requests retry or use Elixir fallback.
scenarios.data_safety: BEAM stays alive and no partial durable state is acknowledged.

scenarios.expected_degradation: Telemetry samples/drops within local bounds without blocking workload.
scenarios.user_visible_effect: No functional outage; operators receive local degraded signal.
scenarios.data_safety: Telemetry loss cannot alter authorization or durable state.

scenarios.expected_degradation: Artifact/log retention stops growth and background admission reduces.
scenarios.user_visible_effect: Optional work pauses before disk reserve is consumed.
scenarios.data_safety: Database and backup integrity remain protected; no deletion of authoritative data.

scenarios.expected_degradation: The owning supervisor restarts the bounded process.
scenarios.user_visible_effect: A narrow session/partition may reconnect or retry.
scenarios.data_safety: Runtime state rebuilds from PostgreSQL authority.

scenarios.expected_degradation: Partition actors restart under bounded admission.
scenarios.user_visible_effect: Affected active entities briefly reconnect or rebuild.
scenarios.data_safety: No in-memory state is treated as durable authority.

scenarios.expected_degradation: Health gate refuses public traffic enablement and automatically deactivates the failed first release.
scenarios.user_visible_effect: V1 remains unavailable; no public route is enabled.
scenarios.data_safety: Database remains forward-only and the exact boot-volume restore point remains available.

scenarios.expected_degradation: Admission drains, public traffic is disabled, and the first V1 release is stopped and deselected.
scenarios.user_visible_effect: Service is unavailable until the release is reactivated or the boot-volume restore procedure completes.
scenarios.data_safety: No down migration runs; PostgreSQL remains authoritative and infrastructure restore evidence is exact-SHA.

scenarios.expected_degradation: systemd restarts PostgreSQL/PgBouncer/runtime in declared dependency order.
scenarios.user_visible_effect: Single-node outage during reboot; clients reconnect afterward.
scenarios.data_safety: Recovery relies on PostgreSQL/WAL and retained release artifacts; no HA claim.
